title: A Case For Host Code Guided GPU Data Race Detector
authors: Ajay Nayak, Anubhab Ghosh, Arkaprava Basu
abstract: Data races in GPU programs pose a threat to the reliability of GPU-accelerated software stacks. Prior works proposed various dynamic (runtime) and static (compile-time) techniques to detect races in GPU programs. However, dynamic techniques often miss critical races, as they require the races to manifest during testing. While static ones can catch such races, they often generate numerous false alarms by conservatively assuming values of variables/parameters that cannot ever occur during any execution of the program. We make a key observation that the host (CPU) code that launches GPU kernels contains crucial semantic information about the values that the GPU kernel's parameters can take during execution. Harnessing this hitherto overlooked information helps accurately detect data races in GPU kernel code. We create HGRD, a new state-of-the-art static analysis technique that performs a holistic analysis of both CPU and GPU code to accurately detect a broad set of true races while minimizing false alarms. While SOTA dynamic techniques, such as iGUARD, miss many true races, HGRD misses none. On the other hand, static techniques such as GPUVerify and FaialAA raise tens of false alarms, where HGRD raises none.
title_zh: 一种面向宿主代码引导的GPU数据竞争检测器的案例
abstract_zh: GPU程序中的数据竞争对GPU加速软件栈的可靠性构成了威胁。以往的研究提出了多种动态（运行时）和静态（编译时）技术来检测GPU程序中的竞争。然而，动态技术常常会遗漏关键的竞争问题，因为它们要求竞争必须在测试过程中实际发生才能被发现。而虽然静态分析能够捕获这类竞争，但由于保守地假设了变量/参数的取值（这些取值在程序任何执行过程中实际上都不可能发生），往往会产生大量误报。我们发现一个关键点：启动GPU内核的主机（CPU）代码中包含了关于GPU内核参数在执行期间可能取值的重要语义信息。利用这一此前被忽视的信息，有助于更准确地检测GPU内核代码中的数据竞争。为此，我们提出了HGRD——一种新的最先进的静态分析技术，它对CPU和GPU代码进行整体分析，能够在准确检测广泛真实竞争的同时，最大限度地减少误报。与最先进的动态技术（如iGUARD）会遗漏许多真实竞争不同，HGRD不会遗漏任何真实竞争；而像GPUVerify和FaialAA等静态技术会产生数十个误报，HGRD则完全不产生误报。

title: Can Large Language Models Model Programs Formally?
authors: Zhiyong Chen, Jialun Cao, Jiarong Wu, Chang Xu, Shing-Chi Cheung
abstract: In the digital age, ensuring the correctness, safety, and reliability of software through formal verification is paramount, particularly as software increasingly underpins critical infrastructure. Formal verification, split into theorem proving and model checking, provides a feasible and reliable path. Unlike theorem proving, which yields notable advances, model checking has been less focused due to the difficulty of automatic program modeling. To fill this gap, we introduce Model-Bench, a benchmark and an accompanying pipeline for evaluating and improving LLMs' program modeling capability by modeling Python programs into verification-ready model checking specifications checkable by its accompanying model checker. Model-Bench comprises 400 Python programs derived from three well-known benchmarks (HumanEval, MBPP, and LiveCodeBench). Our extensive experiments reveal significant limitations in LLMs' program modeling and further provide inspiring directions.
title_zh: 大语言模型能否对程序进行形式化建模？
abstract_zh: 在数字时代，随着软件日益成为关键基础设施的基础，通过形式化验证来确保软件的正确性、安全性和可靠性变得至关重要。形式化验证分为定理证明和模型检测两条路径，为实现这一目标提供了可行且可靠的方法。与取得显著进展的定理证明不同，由于程序自动建模的困难，模型检测一直较少受到关注。为填补这一空白，我们提出了 Model-Bench——一个用于评估和提升大语言模型（LLM）程序建模能力的基准测试及配套流程。该流程将Python程序建模为可由配套模型检测工具验证的规格说明。Model-Bench 包含从三个知名基准（HumanEval、MBPP 和 LiveCodeBench）衍生出的 400 个 Python 程序。大量实验揭示了当前大语言模型在程序建模方面存在的显著局限性，同时也指明了富有启发性的改进方向。

title: Preferential Bayesian Optimization with Crash Feedback
authors: Johanna Menn, David Stenger, Sebastian Trimpe
abstract: Bayesian optimization is a popular black-box optimization method for parameter learning in control and robotics. It typically requires an objective function that reflects the user's optimization goal. However, in practical applications, this objective function is often inaccessible due to complex or unmeasurable performance metrics. Preferential Bayesian optimization (PBO) overcomes this limitation by leveraging human feedback through pairwise comparisons, eliminating the need for explicit performance quantification. When applying PBO to hardware systems, such as in quadcopter control, crashes can cause time-consuming experimental resets, wear and tear, or otherwise undesired outcomes. Standard PBO methods cannot incorporate feedback from such crashed experiments, resulting in the exploration of parameters that frequently lead to experimental crashes. We thus introduce CrashPBO, a user-friendly mechanism that enables users to both express preferences and report crashes during the optimization process. Benchmarking on synthetic functions shows that this mechanism reduces crashes by 63% and increases data efficiency. Through experiments on three robotics platforms, we demonstrate the wide applicability and transferability of CrashPBO, highlighting that it provides a flexible, user-friendly framework for parameter learning with human feedback on preferences and crashes.
title_zh: 带有崩溃反馈的偏好贝叶斯优化
abstract_zh: 贝叶斯优化是控制与机器人领域中一种流行的黑箱优化方法，用于参数学习。它通常需要一个能够反映用户优化目标的目标函数。然而在实际应用中，由于性能指标复杂或难以测量，该目标函数往往无法获得。偏好式贝叶斯优化（Preferential Bayesian Optimization, PBO）通过利用人类提供的成对比较反馈，克服了这一限制，从而无需对性能进行显式量化。当将PBO应用于四旋翼飞行器控制等硬件系统时，实验过程中可能发生坠机，导致耗时的实验重置、设备磨损或其他不良后果。传统的PBO方法无法利用来自此类失败实验的反馈，因而容易持续探索那些频繁引发实验失败的参数。为此，我们提出了CrashPBO——一种用户友好的机制，使用户不仅能够表达偏好，还能在优化过程中报告实验失败。在合成函数上的基准测试表明，该机制可将实验失败次数减少63%，并显著提升数据利用效率。通过对三个机器人平台的实验，我们验证了CrashPBO的广泛适用性和可迁移性，表明其为结合人类关于偏好和失败反馈的参数学习提供了一个灵活且易于使用的框架。

title: Assertain: Automated Security Assertion Generation Using Large Language Models
authors: Shams Tarek, Dipayan Saha, Khan Thamid Hasan, Sujan Kumar Saha, Mark Tehranipoor, Farimah Farahmandi
abstract: The increasing complexity of modern system-on-chip designs amplifies hardware security risks and makes manual security property specification a major bottleneck in formal property verification. This paper presents Assertain, an automated framework that integrates RTL design analysis, Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) mapping, and threat model intelligence to automatically generate security properties and executable SystemVerilog Assertions. Assertain leverages large language models with a self-reflection refinement mechanism to ensure both syntactic correctness and semantic consistency. Evaluated on 11 representative hardware designs, Assertain outperforms GPT-5 by 61.22%, 59.49%, and 67.92% in correct assertion generation, unique CWE coverage, and architectural flaw detection, respectively. These results demonstrate that Assertain significantly expands vulnerability coverage, improves assertion quality, and reduces manual effort in hardware security verification.
title_zh: Assertain：基于大语言模型的自动化安全断言生成
abstract_zh: 现代片上系统设计的日益复杂性加剧了硬件安全风险，也使得手动进行安全属性规范成为形式化属性验证中的主要瓶颈。本文提出了Assertain——一种自动化框架，该框架集成了RTL设计分析、通用缺陷枚举（CWE）映射和威胁模型智能，能够自动生成安全属性和可执行的SystemVerilog断言。Assertain利用具备自我反思优化机制的大语言模型，确保生成断言在语法上的正确性和语义上的一致性。在11个代表性硬件设计上的评估结果表明，Assertain在正确断言生成、唯一CWE覆盖范围以及架构级缺陷检测方面分别比GPT-5高出61.22%、59.49%和67.92%。实验结果表明，Assertain显著扩展了漏洞覆盖范围，提升了断言质量，并大幅减少了硬件安全验证中的人工投入。

title: AI-Assisted Hardware Security Verification: A Survey and AI Accelerator Case Study
authors: Khan Thamid Hasan, Md Ajoad Hasan, Nashmin Alam, Md. Touhidul Islam, Upoma Das, Farimah Farahmandi
abstract: As hardware systems grow in complexity, security verification must keep up with them. Recently, artificial intelligence (AI) and large language models (LLMs) have started to play an important role in automating several stages of the verification workflow by helping engineers analyze designs, reason about potential threats, and generate verification artifacts. This survey synthesizes recent advances in AI-assisted hardware security verification and organizes the literature along key stages of the workflow: asset identification, threat modeling, security test-plan generation, simulation-driven analysis, formal verification, and countermeasure reasoning. To illustrate how these techniques can be applied in practice, we present a case study using the open-source NVIDIA Deep Learning Accelerator (NVDLA), a representative modern hardware design. Throughout this study, we emphasize that while AI/LLM-based automation can significantly accelerate verification tasks, its outputs must remain grounded in simulation evidence, formal reasoning, and benchmark-driven evaluation to ensure trustworthy hardware security assurance.
title_zh: AI辅助的硬件安全验证：一项综述及AI加速器案例研究
abstract_zh: 随着硬件系统复杂性的不断增加，安全验证必须同步跟进。近年来，人工智能（AI）和大语言模型（LLM）开始在自动化验证流程的多个阶段中发挥重要作用，帮助工程师分析设计、推理潜在威胁并生成验证产物。本文综述了AI辅助硬件安全验证领域的最新进展，并根据验证工作流的关键阶段对现有文献进行了系统梳理：资产识别、威胁建模、安全测试计划生成、基于仿真的分析、形式化验证以及防护措施推理。为了展示这些技术在实际中的应用，我们以开源的英伟达深度学习加速器（NVDLA）为例进行案例研究，该设计代表了现代硬件系统的典型特征。在整个研究过程中，我们强调，尽管基于AI/LLM的自动化可显著加速验证任务，但其输出结果必须以仿真证据、形式化推理和基于基准的评估为基础，以确保实现可信的硬件安全保障。

title: (PAC-)Learning state machines from data streams: A generic strategy and an improved heuristic (Extended version)
abstract: This is an extended version of our publication Learning state machines from data streams: A generic strategy and an improved heuristic, International Conference on Grammatical Inference (ICGI) 2023, Rabat, Morocco. It has been extended with a formal proof on PAC-bounds, and the discussion and analysis of a similar approach has been moved from the appendix and is now a full Section. State machines models are models that simulate the behavior of discrete event systems, capable of representing systems such as software systems, network interactions, and control systems, and have been researched extensively. The nature of most learning algorithms however is the assumption that all data be available at the beginning of the algorithm, and little research has been done in learning state machines from streaming data. In this paper, we want to close this gap further by presenting a generic method for learning state machines from data streams, as well as a merge heuristic that uses sketches to account for incomplete prefix trees. We implement our approach in an open-source state merging library and compare it with existing methods. We show the effectiveness of our approach with respect to run-time, memory consumption, and quality of results on a well known open dataset. Additionally, we provide a formal analysis of our algorithm, showing that it is capable of learning within the PAC framework, and show a theoretical improvement to increase run-time, without sacrificing correctness of the algorithm in larger sample sizes.
title_zh: 从数据流中（PAC）学习状态机：一种通用策略与改进的启发式方法（扩展版本）
abstract_zh: 这是我们发表在2023年摩洛哥拉巴特举行的国际语法推断会议（ICGI）上的论文《从数据流中学习状态机：一种通用策略与改进的启发式方法》的扩展版本。本文新增了关于PAC界限的正式证明，并将原附录中对类似方法的讨论与分析提升为一个完整的章节。状态机模型能够模拟离散事件系统的行为，可用于表示软件系统、网络交互和控制系统等，在学术界已得到广泛研究。然而，大多数学习算法的本质假设是所有数据在算法开始时就已全部可用，而针对从数据流中学习状态机的研究则相对较少。本文旨在进一步弥补这一空白，提出了一种从数据流中学习状态机的通用方法，以及一种利用“草图”（sketches）来处理不完整前缀树的合并启发式策略。我们将该方法实现于一个开源的状态合并库中，并与现有方法进行了比较。通过一个知名的公开数据集，我们验证了该方法在运行时间、内存消耗和结果质量方面的有效性。此外，我们还对算法进行了形式化分析，证明其能够在PAC（Probably Approximately Correct）学习框架下进行学习，并提出了一种理论上的优化方案，可在不影响算法正确性的前提下，提升大规模样本中的运行效率。

title: TestDecision: Sequential Test Suite Generation via Greedy Optimization and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Guoqing Wang, Chengran Yang, Xiaoxuan Zhou, Zeyu Sun, Bo Wang, David Lo, Dan Hao
abstract: With the rapid evolution of LLMs, automated software testing is witnessing a paradigm shift. While proprietary models like GPT-4o demonstrate impressive capabilities, their high deployment costs and data privacy concerns make open-source LLMs the practical imperative for many academic and industrial scenarios. In the field of automated test generation, it has evolved to iterative workflows to construct test suites based on LLMs. When utilizing open-source LLMs, we empirically observe they lack a suite-level perspective, suffering from structural myopia-failing to generate new tests with large marginal gain based on the current covered status. In this paper, from the perspective of sequences, we formalize test suite generation as a MDP and demonstrate that its objective exhibits monotone submodularity, which enables an effective relaxation of this NP-hard global optimization into a tractable step-wise greedy procedure. Guided by this insight, we propose TestDecision, which transforms LLMs into neural greedy experts. TestDecision consists of two synergistic components: (1) an inference framework which implements test suite construction following a step-wise greedy strategy; and (2) a training pipeline of reinforcement learning which equips the base LLM with sequential test generation ability to maximize marginal gain. Comprehensive evaluations on the ULT benchmark demonstrate that TestDecision significantly outperforms existing advanced methods. It brings an improvement between 38.15-52.37% in branch coverage and 298.22-558.88% in execution pass rate over all base models, achieving a comparable performance on 7B backbone with a much larger proprietary LLM GPT-5.2. Furthermore, TestDecision can find 58.43-95.45% more bugs than vanilla base LLMs and exhibit superior generalization on LiveCodeBench, proving its capability to construct high-quality test suites.
title_zh: 测试决策：通过贪婪优化与强化学习实现的顺序测试套件生成
abstract_zh:
随着大语言模型（LLM）的快速发展，自动化软件测试正经历一场范式变革。尽管GPT-4o等闭源模型展现出强大的能力，但其高昂的部署成本和数据隐私问题使得开源LLM在众多学术与工业场景中成为更实际的选择。在自动化测试生成领域，研究已发展为基于LLM的迭代式工作流来构建测试套件。然而我们通过实验发现，当使用开源LLM时，它们往往缺乏对测试套件整体的宏观视角，存在“结构近视”问题——即无法根据当前代码覆盖状态生成具有较大边际增益的新测试用例。

本文从序列建模的角度出发，将测试套件生成形式化为一个马尔可夫决策过程（MDP），并证明其目标函数具有单调子模性（monotone submodularity）。这一性质使得我们可以将原本NP难的全局优化问题有效松弛为可操作的逐级贪心策略。基于这一洞察，我们提出了TestDecision框架，将LLM转化为神经贪心专家。TestDecision包含两个协同组件：（1）推理框架，采用逐级贪心策略实现测试套件的逐步构建；（2）基于强化学习的训练流程，赋予基础LLM以最大化边际增益为目标的序列化测试生成能力。

在ULT基准上的综合评估表明，TestDecision显著优于现有先进方法：在分支覆盖率上提升38.15%-52.37%，执行通过率提高298.22%-558.88%。尤其值得注意的是，基于7B参数规模骨干模型的TestDecision，性能已接近远为庞大的闭源模型GPT-5.2。此外，TestDecision相比原始基础LLM能多发现58.43%-95.45%的缺陷，并在LiveCodeBench上表现出优异的泛化能力，充分验证了其构建高质量测试套件的有效性。

title: Are Benchmark Tests Strong Enough? Mutation-Guided Diagnosis and Augmentation of Regression Suites
authors: Chenglin Li, Yisen Xu, Zehao Wang, Shin Hwei Tan, Tse-Hsun, Chen
abstract: Benchmarks driven by test suites, notably SWE-bench, have become the de facto standard for measuring the effectiveness of automated issue-resolution agents: a generated patch is accepted whenever it passes the accompanying regression tests. In practice, however, insufficiently strong test suites can admit plausible yet semantically incorrect patches, inflating reported success rates. We introduce STING, a framework for targeted test augmentation that uses semantically altered program variants as diagnostic stressors to uncover and repair weaknesses in benchmark regression suites. Variants of the ground-truth patch that still pass the existing tests reveal under-constrained behaviors; these gaps then guide the generation of focused regression tests. A generated test is retained only if it (i) passes on the ground-truth patch, (ii) fails on at least one variant that survived the original suite, and (iii) remains valid under behavior-preserving transformations designed to guard against overfitting. Applied to SWE-bench Verified, STING finds that 77% of instances contain at least one surviving variant. STING produces 1,014 validated tests spanning 211 instances and increases patch-region line and branch coverage by 10.8% and 9.5%, respectively. Re-assessing the top-10 repair agents with the strengthened suites lowers their resolved rates by 4.2%-9.0%, revealing that a substantial share of previously passing patches exploit weaknesses in the benchmark tests rather than faithfully implementing the intended fix. These results underscore that reliable benchmark evaluation depends not only on patch generation, but equally on test adequacy.
title_zh: 基准测试足够强大吗？基于变异的回归测试套件诊断与增强
abstract_zh: 由测试套件驱动的基准测试，尤其是SWE-bench，已成为衡量自动化问题修复代理有效性的事实标准：只要生成的补丁通过配套的回归测试，即被视为可接受。然而在实践中，测试套件若不够充分，可能会接纳看似合理但语义错误的补丁，从而虚高报告的成功率。为此，我们提出了STING——一种面向测试增强的框架，该框架利用语义上被修改的程序变体作为诊断性压力源，以发现并修复基准回归测试套件中的薄弱环节。那些与真实补丁语义不同但仍能通过现有测试的变体，揭示了测试约束不足的行为；这些漏洞进而指导生成针对性更强的回归测试。一个生成的测试仅在满足以下三个条件时才会被保留：（i）在真实补丁上通过；（ii）至少在一个通过原始测试套件的变体上失败；（iii）在经过旨在防止过拟合的行为保持型变换下仍保持有效性。将STING应用于SWE-bench Verified时发现，77%的实例中至少存在一个“幸存”的变体。STING共生成了1,014个经验证的测试，覆盖211个实例，使补丁区域的代码行覆盖率和分支覆盖率分别提升了10.8%和9.5%。使用增强后的测试套件重新评估排名前十的修复代理，其解决率下降了4.2%至9.0%，表明此前大量通过的补丁实际上利用了基准测试中的漏洞，而非正确实现了预期修复。这些结果凸显出，可靠的基准评估不仅依赖于补丁生成能力，同样也取决于测试的充分性。

title: R2H: Rowhammer attack in presence of randomized LLC
authors: Gaurav Gaurav, Prathamesh Nitin Tanksale, Venkata Kalyan Tavva
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: To defend against conflict-based attacks at the Last Level Cache (LLC), randomization is proposed. At the DRAM, to induce the Rowhammer attack, attackers generate memory requests that bypass the on-chip caches and hammer selective DRAM rows. The impact of the set-eviction based Rowhammer attack in the presence of randomized LLC is unexplored till date, and we find it to be ineffective. We propose R2H, a Rowhammer attack that works even with randomized LLC designs. By targeting a subset of cache sets, R2H drives the LLC miss rate comparable to that of conventional caches, in addition these misses are concentrated to specific DRAM rows, enabling a successful Rowhammer attack despite randomized set mappings.
title_zh: R2H：在存在随机化LLC的情况下进行Rowhammer攻击
abstract_zh: 为了防御针对末级缓存（Last Level Cache, LLC）的基于冲突的攻击，研究者提出了随机化方法。在DRAM层面，攻击者通过生成绕过片上缓存的内存请求来实施Rowhammer攻击，反复激活特定的DRAM行。迄今为止，集替换式Rowhammer攻击在采用随机化LLC情况下的影响尚未被探索，而我们发现此类攻击在该环境下是无效的。为此，我们提出了一种名为R2H的新型Rowhammer攻击方法，即使在LLC采用随机化设计的情况下仍可成功实施。R2H通过针对缓存中的一部分集合，使得LLC的缺失率与传统缓存相当，并且这些缺失集中在特定的DRAM行上，从而在存在随机化集映射的情况下依然能够成功实现Rowhammer攻击。

title: Hardware-Software Co-Design of Post-Quantum FALCON Digital Signature Scheme with RISC-V
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: FALCON is a lattice-based digital signature scheme recently selected as NIST post-quantum cryptography standard. FALCON is well known for its fast verification support along with small key and signature sizes. However, implementation of the FALCON signature algorithm in resource-constrained edge computing systems is challenging due to the requirement of complex hashing, floating point arithmetic and polynomial transformation computations. In this work, we demonstrate resource-efficient hardware acceleration of FALCON signature generation and verification by performing hardware-software co-design of its computationally expensive functions with a light-weight 32-bit Vex RISC-V processor core with accompanying custom instructions. We propose Custom Function Units compatible with Vex RISC-V to accelerate Keccak permutations and polynomial transformations, along with a constant-time implementation of IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic. Our design achieves ≈ 4.3× speedup for signature verification and ≈ 1.6× speedup for signature generation compared to the baseline software on Vex RISC-V for both parameter sets of FALCON, when implemented on AMD Xilinx UltraScale+ FPGA. We also present an efficient verification-only variant of our design suitable for resource-constrained applications.
title_zh: 基于RISC-V的后量子FALCON数字签名方案的软硬件协同设计
abstract_zh: FALCON 是一种基于格的数字签名方案，最近被选为 NIST 后量子密码学标准。FALCON 以其快速的验证能力以及较小的密钥和签名尺寸而著称。然而，由于需要复杂的哈希运算、浮点算术和多项式变换计算，在资源受限的边缘计算系统中实现 FALCON 签名算法具有挑战性。在本研究中，我们通过硬件-软件协同设计的方法，针对其计算开销较大的功能模块，结合轻量级 32 位 Vex RISC-V 处理器核心及配套的自定义指令，实现了资源高效的 FALCON 签名生成与验证硬件加速。我们提出了兼容 Vex RISC-V 的自定义功能单元，用于加速 Keccak 置换和多项式变换，并实现了符合 IEEE 754 标准的恒定时间浮点运算。当在 AMD Xilinx UltraScale+ FPGA 上实现时，与基于 Vex RISC-V 的纯软件基线相比，我们的设计在 FALCON 的两组参数下分别实现了约 4.3 倍的验证速度提升和约 1.6 倍的签名生成速度提升。此外，我们还提出了一种适用于资源受限应用的高效仅验证版本设计。

title: Digital Twins Reimagined: Zero-Day LLM-Powered Moving Target Defense In-Depth for Real-Time CPS
authors: Rajarshi Mukherjee, Mohamed Azab, Thidapat Chantem
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) revolution has profoundly transformed modern industries by embedding connected digital devices across critical sectors such as energy, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing. Such interconnected devices together form Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS), complex structures that enable computation to directly control physical processes. These systems increasingly rely on automation and real-time control to enhance efficiency, productivity, and safety. However, this growing dependence on connectivity has significantly expanded the attack surface, exposing mission-critical infrastructure to sophisticated cyber threats. Among the most dangerous are zero-day vulnerabilities, which are unknown flaws in software or firmware, and zero-day design flaws, which are deeply embedded architectural weaknesses often undetectable by conventional verification methods. Traditional defense mechanisms, largely based on static rules or known threat signatures, are ill-equipped to detect these stealthy and evolving threats - especially in real-time systems, where even minor delays in detection or response can result in catastrophic outcomes. To overcome these limitations, this work presents a transformative security framework that leverages the reasoning and generative capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to construct an intelligent, redundant digital twin of the operational system. Unlike traditional replicas, the LLM-driven digital twin is logically and architecturally isolated, making it inaccessible to attackers targeting the primary system. Crucially, the twin is not a static copy: it is dynamically synthesized and continuously restructured by the LLM to ensure a heterogeneous and evolving codebase. This ensures functional alignment with the physical system while introducing deliberate software diversity, thereby increasing attacker uncertainty and resilience to shared exploits. The central hypothesis of the framework is that semantic deviations between the decisions or behaviors of the digital twin and its physical counterpart can serve as reliable indicators of anomalous activity—whether stemming from external attacks or latent design flaws. By monitoring this divergence, the system facilitates the early detection of zero-day threats and supports real-time mitigation strategies, thereby significantly strengthening the resilience of critical infrastructure. Implementation results demonstrated the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed approach.
title_zh: 数字孪生重构：面向实时信息物理系统的零日LLM驱动移动目标深度防御
abstract_zh:
物联网（IoT）革命通过在能源、制药和制造等关键领域广泛部署互联的数字设备，深刻地改变了现代工业格局。这些相互连接的设备共同构成了信息物理系统（Cyber-Physical Systems, CPS），即一种复杂的结构，能够将计算过程直接用于控制物理流程。此类系统日益依赖自动化与实时控制，以提升效率、生产力和安全性。然而，对网络连接的不断依赖也显著扩大了攻击面，使关键任务基础设施面临日益复杂的网络威胁。其中最危险的威胁包括“零日漏洞”——即软件或固件中尚未被发现的缺陷，以及“零日设计缺陷”——即深植于系统架构中的薄弱环节，通常难以通过传统验证方法检测出来。传统的防御机制主要基于静态规则或已知威胁特征，难以应对这些隐蔽且不断演变的威胁，尤其是在实时系统中，检测或响应上的微小延迟都可能导致灾难性后果。

为克服上述局限，本文提出了一种变革性的安全框架，该框架利用大语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）的推理与生成能力，构建一个智能且具备冗余特性的数字孪生系统。与传统副本不同，由LLM驱动的数字孪生在逻辑和架构上完全隔离，攻击者无法通过入侵主系统而触及该孪生体。更重要的是，该孪生系统并非静态复制：它由LLM动态生成并持续重构，从而确保代码库具有异构性和演化性。这既保证了与物理系统的功能一致性，又引入了有意的软件多样性，进而增加攻击者的不确定性，并增强系统对通用漏洞利用的抵御能力。

该框架的核心假设是：数字孪生与其物理实体在决策或行为上的语义偏差，可作为异常活动的可靠指标——无论这种异常源于外部攻击还是潜在的设计缺陷。通过监控这种偏差，系统能够实现对零日威胁的早期预警，并支持实时缓解策略，从而显著提升关键基础设施的韧性。实验结果表明，所提出的方法在有效性与效率方面均表现出色。

title: Towards Secure RISC-V Microarchitecture: Vulnerability Classes and Defenses
authors: Mahreen Khan, Maria Mushtaq, Renaud Pacalet, Ludovic Apvrille
abstract: RRISC-V is an open instruction set architecture that enables a wide range of processor implementations across academia and industry. While its architectural simplicity and extensibility support rapid innovation, RISC-V processors exhibit microarchitectural behaviors that give rise to side-channel and transient-execution vulnerabilities similar to those observed in other high-performance CPU architectures. This paper presents a structured taxonomy of microarchitectural vulnerabilities in RISC-V processors, organized into five classes based on the affected hardware components and dominant sources of leakage: cache-based channels, speculative and transient execution, branch prediction structures, memory and address translation subsystems, and observation through performance monitoring mechanisms. For each class, we examine representative vulnerabilities and documented attacks on existing RISC-V implementations and research prototypes, and analyze the underlying microarchitectural mechanisms that enable information leakage. We further study countermeasures applicable to each vulnerability class, covering both RISC-V-specific proposals and adaptations of mechanisms previously developed for x86 and ARM platforms. The surveyed defenses are systematically analyzed in terms of security coverage, performance overhead, and implementation complexity, highlighting both their strengths and practical limitations. The resulting taxonomy and comparative analysis aim to support RISC-V architects, system designers, and software developers in reasoning about microarchitectural risks and mitigation trade-offs during system design.
title_zh: 面向安全的RISC-V微架构：漏洞类别与防御措施
abstract_zh: RRISC-V 是一种开放的指令集架构，支持学术界和工业界广泛多样的处理器实现。尽管其架构设计简洁且具有良好的可扩展性，有利于快速创新，但RISC-V处理器在微架构层面的行为仍会引发旁路信道和瞬态执行漏洞，这类问题与在其他高性能CPU架构中观察到的漏洞相似。本文提出了一种针对RISC-V处理器微架构漏洞的系统化分类方法，根据受影响的硬件组件及主要信息泄露来源，将漏洞划分为五类：基于缓存的信道、推测与瞬态执行、分支预测结构、内存与地址转换子系统，以及通过性能监控机制进行观测的攻击途径。对于每一类漏洞，我们考察了现有RISC-V实现和研究原型中具有代表性的漏洞实例与已记录的攻击方式，并分析了导致信息泄露的根本微架构机制。此外，我们还研究了适用于每类漏洞的防御措施，涵盖专为RISC-V提出的方案，以及从x86和ARM平台借鉴并适配的防护机制。本文对所调研的各类防御手段从安全性覆盖范围、性能开销和实现复杂度等方面进行了系统分析，揭示了它们的优势与实际应用中的局限性。最终形成的分类体系与对比分析旨在帮助RISC-V架构师、系统设计者和软件开发者在系统设计过程中更好地评估微架构风险及其缓解措施之间的权衡。

title: Quantitative Evaluation of Hazard Mitigation Strategies in a 5-Stage RISC-V Pipeline: A Benchmark-Driven Analysis Using Fibonacci and Prime Sieve
authors: Kushagra Agrawal, Anindita Basak, Abhishek Raj, Aakarsh Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: This paper presents a quantitative deep dive into the hazard mitigation strategies of a custom five-stage (IF–ID–EX–MEM–WB) pipelined RISC-V processor. The architecture was built with a dedicated hazard detection unit and full data forwarding to effectively handle Read-After-Write (RAW) dependencies and control flow challenges. To evaluate performance, we conducted cycle-accurate simulations using targeted benchmarks: the Fibonacci sequence to stress data forwarding and the Prime Number Sieve to assess branch handling efficiency.The results from rigorous testing showcase the architecture’s robustness. Complete success in hazard mitigation was confirmed by recording zero data stalls across all workloads, demonstrating perfect resolution of RAW dependencies. The processor achieved an efficient Cycles Per Instruction (CPI) of 1.62 on computationally intensive tasks. Even for the branch-heavy Prime Sieve, it maintained a predictable CPI of 2.90. This performance reflects a precise one-to-one correlation between branch instructions and stalls, indicating a deterministic one-cycle branch penalty. These findings validate a sound architectural design and highlight the processor’s suitability for educational platforms and embedded systems where predictable performance is essential.
title_zh: 基于斐波那契与素数筛法基准测试的五级RISC-V流水线中灾害缓解策略的定量评估：一项以基准驱动的分析
abstract_zh:
本文对一种定制的五级流水线（IF-ID-EX-MEM-WB）RISC-V处理器中的冒险消除策略进行了定量深入分析。该处理器架构配备了专用的冒险检测单元和完整的数据前递机制，能够有效处理写后读（RAW）数据依赖以及控制流带来的挑战。为评估其性能，我们使用针对性的基准测试程序进行了周期精确的仿真：采用斐波那契数列程序重点测试数据前递能力，利用素数筛法程序评估分支处理效率。

严格的测试结果表明该架构具有出色的鲁棒性。在所有工作负载下均未出现数据相关停顿，确认了RAW依赖被完全消除。在计算密集型任务中，处理器实现了1.62的高效每条指令周期数（CPI）。即使在分支密集的素数筛法程序中，CPI仍稳定保持在2.90。这一表现反映出分支指令与停顿之间存在精确的一比一对应关系，表明其具有确定性的单周期分支惩罚。上述结果验证了该处理器架构设计的合理性，突显其在需要性能可预测性的教学平台和嵌入式系统中的适用性。

title: SAMURAI: Runtime Attack Detection in AI Accelerators Using AI Performance Counters
authors: Habibur Rahaman, Atri Chatterjee, Swarup Bhunia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Artificial Intelligence
abstract: AI accelerators, which improve performance for diverse AI tasks, are becoming increasingly vulnerable to various adversarial attacks. These attacks, particularly Evasion attacks, introduce small, imperceptible perturbations to input data, leading to incorrect model predictions. Detecting such attacks is challenging because of their subtle nature. To address this, we propose SAMURAI, a framework designed for runtime attack detection in AI accelerators. SAMURAI features an array of AI Performance Counters (APCs) to monitor the dynamic behavior of AI models, in the same way as Hardware Performance Counters (HPCs) track hardware-related activities in modern microprocessors. It also integrates a lightweight on-chip Machine Learning (ML) engine, called TANTO (Trained Anomaly Inspection through Trace Observation). The APCs track low-level hardware events during AI processing, while TANTO concurrently analyzes these data to detect security threats and ensure secure AI operation. This approach enables runtime identification of potential threats without relying on traditional software-based methods. The study applies various adversarial attacks to both image and audio signals to assess the robustness of SAMURAI for several representative AI models. For audio input, both raw audio data and their 2D Mel-spectrogram representations are examined. Experimental results on image and audio datasets demonstrate that SAMURAI provides high resilience against adversarial attacks with a detection accuracy of 98%. It incurs a modest overhead of < 3% in the total energy, < 4% in area, and < 10% in latency over the original inference time. The source code for SAMURAI is publicly available on GitHub.1
title_zh: 武士： 利用AI性能计数器在AI加速器中进行运行时攻击检测
abstract_zh:
用于加速各类人工智能（AI）任务的AI加速器正日益面临多种对抗性攻击的威胁。其中，逃避攻击（Evasion attacks）通过对输入数据添加微小且难以察觉的扰动，导致模型产生错误预测，尤其具有破坏性。由于此类攻击极为隐蔽，检测难度较大。为此，我们提出了一种名为SAMURAI的框架，专为AI加速器在运行时进行攻击检测而设计。SAMURAI配备了一系列AI性能计数器（APCs），用以监控AI模型的动态行为，其作用类似于现代微处理器中利用硬件性能计数器（HPCs）追踪硬件活动的方式。此外，该框架还集成了一个轻量级的片上机器学习（ML）引擎——TANTO（基于轨迹观测的训练异常检测）。APC负责在AI处理过程中跟踪底层硬件事件，而TANTO则同步分析这些数据，以识别安全威胁并保障AI系统的安全运行。该方法能够在不依赖传统软件检测手段的前提下，实现对潜在威胁的实时识别。

本研究在多个典型AI模型上应用了多种对抗性攻击，针对图像和音频信号评估SAMURAI的鲁棒性。对于音频输入，同时考察了原始音频数据及其二维梅尔频谱图（Mel-spectrogram）表示形式。在图像与音频数据集上的实验结果表明，SAMURAI对对抗攻击具备高度韧性，检测准确率高达98%。同时，其整体开销较低：总能耗增加不足3%，面积开销低于4%，延迟增加不到原始推理时间的10%。SAMURAI的源代码已公开发布于GitHub平台。

title: Securing Approximate Communication in NoC-based Systems
authors: Tarun Sharma, Deepank Grover, Sujay Deb
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Network-on-Chip (NoC) architectures consume a significant portion of system power, and Approximate Communication (AC) has been proposed to reduce this overhead by exploiting the error tolerance of many applications. However, hardware modifications that enable AC also introduce new security vulnerabilities. In particular, a malicious adversary can target the approximation control structures to increase errors beyond the acceptable range, resulting in degraded quality of application output. In this work, we present an attack model of such a Hardware Trojan (HT) and propose a lightweight framework to mitigate its effect. The framework splits the approximation information between the NoC interface and the L1 cache, using application-specific sensitivity profiling. Preliminary evaluation on the Blackscholes benchmark shows that the framework limits security vulnerabilities while retaining most of the performance benefits of AC.
title_zh: 基于片上网络系统的近似通信安全
abstract_zh: 片上网络（NoC）架构消耗了系统中相当大比例的功耗，而近似通信（AC）通过利用许多应用对误差的容忍性，被提出用于降低这一开销。然而，实现AC所需的硬件修改也引入了新的安全漏洞。特别是，恶意攻击者可针对近似控制结构发起攻击，使错误率超出可接受范围，从而导致应用程序输出质量严重下降。本文提出了一种此类硬件木马（HT）的攻击模型，并设计了一个轻量级的防御框架以减轻其影响。该框架利用应用特定的敏感性分析，将近似信息拆分存储于NoC接口与L1缓存之间。基于Blackscholes基准的初步评估结果表明，该框架在保留AC大部分性能优势的同时，有效限制了安全漏洞。
